skill.name: ui-ux-pro-max
skill.description: UI/UX design assistant that searches bundled guidance for styles, color palettes, typography, charts, landing pages, products, UX rules, and stack-specific frontend patterns.
skill.summary: Search UI/UX references and stack guidance to plan, review, and improve polished interfaces.
skill.author: nextlevelbuilder

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many severe patterns, but review found the flagged content is primarily bundled CSV reference data, documentation URLs, color hex values, and labeled good or bad code examples. The executable Python scripts perform local CSV search and formatting only; no evidence found of credential exfiltration, command-and-control behavior, prompt injection, or malicious intent.

low_findings.title: Reference URLs Misclassified as Network Behavior
low_findings.description: Hardcoded URLs are documentation links and Google Fonts import examples stored in CSV reference data. The reviewed Python scripts do not make HTTP requests or send data to these URLs.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited rows are static reference content, and the executable code only reads local CSV files. No network client or exfiltration path was found in scripts/core.py or scripts/search.py.

low_findings.title: Secret and Environment Terms Are Labeled Guidance
low_findings.description: Environment variable and secret strings appear inside stack best-practice rows that explain safe configuration patterns and unsafe anti-patterns. They are not read from the host environment by the skill code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The rows are CSV guidance with Do and Do not columns. The Python scripts do not access process.env or environment files.

low_findings.title: Command Examples Are Documentation Workflow, Not Hidden Execution
low_findings.description: The shell, sudo, and PowerShell hits are visible prerequisite and usage commands in SKILL.md. They instruct the assistant how to run the local search script or install Python when missing.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are transparent and task-related, but package installation commands still warrant user awareness. No stealth execution, persistence, or obfuscation was found.

low_findings.title: Dynamic Imports Are Framework Examples
low_findings.description: Dynamic import patterns occur in stack guidance for lazy loading and code splitting. They are not executed by the skill and are presented as frontend implementation examples.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The import expressions are CSV example snippets for common frontend frameworks. No runtime dynamic import occurs in scripts/core.py or scripts/search.py.

low_findings.title: Security Keywords Are Design Vocabulary and Anti-Patterns
low_findings.description: C2, keylogger, and weak-crypto detections are caused by words such as contrast, command palette, keystroke, hex colors, and algorithm names in UI guidance. No evidence found of malware behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review shows benign UI, chart, and search terminology at the cited locations. The BM25 class is a text ranking algorithm, not cryptography.

low_findings.title: Local CSV File Reads Are Expected for Search
low_findings.description: The script builds a data directory path and opens bundled CSV files for local search. This is legitimate filesystem use for the skill purpose and does not read arbitrary user paths.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The filesystem access is real, but paths are derived from the skill data directory and static configuration. No arbitrary file read or sensitive path traversal was found.

content.user_title: Build Polished UI With Design Guidance
content.value_statement: Designing consistent UI across styles, stacks, and product types is hard to do from memory. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code searchable UI/UX references for faster planning and review.
actual_capabilities: Searches bundled UI style guidance, including minimalism, glassmorphism, dark mode, bento grids, and accessibility-first patterns.
Suggests product-specific design direction for SaaS, dashboards, e-commerce, healthcare, finance, portfolios, and service pages.
Returns color palettes, font pairings, chart recommendations, landing-page patterns, and UX checklist guidance.
Provides stack-specific frontend guidance for React, Next.js, Vue, Svelte, SwiftUI, React Native, Flutter, Nuxt, and Tailwind HTML.
Includes a Python CLI search workflow for targeted lookup by domain, keyword, stack, and result count.
limitations: The guidance comes from bundled CSV files and may not reflect the newest framework releases.
Python must be available to use the included search script workflow.
The skill provides design guidance and examples, but it does not visually test generated interfaces by itself.
Some rows include labeled bad examples that require careful interpretation before reuse.

use_cases.title: Plan a product interface direction
use_cases.description: Choose suitable styles, colors, typography, and landing-page structure before asking an AI assistant to build a first version.

use_cases.title: Review frontend implementation quality
use_cases.description: Check a UI against stack-specific patterns, accessibility expectations, layout rules, responsive behavior, and interaction details.

use_cases.title: Create a consistent design system brief
use_cases.description: Generate a practical brief covering palette, type pairing, component style, chart direction, and UX constraints.

prompt_templates.title: Choose a UI direction
prompt_templates.prompt: Use ui-ux-pro-max to recommend a visual direction for a SaaS landing page. Include style, palette, typography, layout, and accessibility notes.

prompt_templates.title: Review an existing screen
prompt_templates.prompt: Use ui-ux-pro-max to review this dashboard UI for hierarchy, spacing, contrast, chart choice, responsive layout, and framework-specific issues.

prompt_templates.title: Build a stack-specific design plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Use ui-ux-pro-max with the Next.js stack guidance to plan a polished analytics product interface with charts, loading states, forms, and navigation.

prompt_templates.title: Synthesize a full design brief
prompt_templates.prompt: Use ui-ux-pro-max across product, style, typography, color, chart, landing, UX, and stack domains to produce a complete implementation brief.

output_examples.input: Design a healthcare appointment booking landing page.
output: Recommended direction: accessible, calm, and trust-focused with healthcare blue and green accents.
Use a clear hero, appointment CTA, doctor credentials, service cards, insurance details, and testimonials.
Prioritize large text, visible focus states, form labels, high contrast, and mobile booking flow clarity.

output_examples.input: Review a React analytics dashboard for usability.
output: Use stable keys, memoized expensive charts, explicit loading and error states, and accessible table alternatives.
Prefer line charts for trends, bar charts for comparisons, and clear labels for color-dependent data.
Check spacing density, keyboard focus, responsive cards, and contrast for muted metrics.

output_examples.input: Create a premium portfolio visual system.
output: Recommended direction: minimal editorial layout with strong typography, restrained motion, and high-quality imagery.
Use a serif heading pair, neutral base colors, one accent color, and generous whitespace.
Avoid heavy effects that distract from case studies and project proof.

best_practices: Search multiple domains before building, especially product, style, color, typography, UX, and stack guidance.
Treat bad examples as warnings and convert recommendations into accessible, tested implementation choices.
Validate generated UI with real viewport checks, keyboard navigation, contrast testing, and framework linting.
anti_patterns: Do not copy every visual trend from the data into one interface.
Do not use framework examples without checking current documentation and project constraints.
Do not prioritize decorative effects over readability, performance, accessibility, or conversion goals.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps AI assistants plan, build, and review UI/UX work using bundled design and frontend reference data.

faq.question: Does it generate finished visual designs automatically?
faq.answer: It provides guidance and implementation direction. The assistant still needs to create, test, and refine the actual interface.

faq.question: Which frontend stacks are covered?
faq.answer: It includes guidance for React, Next.js, Vue, Svelte, SwiftUI, React Native, Flutter, Nuxt, Nuxt UI, and Tailwind HTML.

faq.question: Can it help with accessibility?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes accessibility-focused style guidance, UX rules, chart notes, focus states, contrast, labels, and motion considerations.

faq.question: Why does the audit show risk factors?
faq.answer: The skill contains executable Python search scripts, command examples, URLs, and environment-variable examples in reference data.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. Review found no malicious behavior, but users should treat generated examples as guidance and verify implementation details.
